complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Temporal Consistency via Latent Caching and Overlap Handling in Video Segment Extender
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down this task into subtasks for: (1) designing and implementing disk-based latent frame caching helpers, (2) updating segment save logic to invoke latent cache creation, (3) modifying segment preparation to load and prepend cached latents, (4) implementing robust error handling and cache fallback logic, (5) developing overlap-aware video segment concatenation including ffmpeg integration and crossfade/overlap interplay, (6) comprehensive logging and backward compatibility toggles, (7) writing unit and integration tests in designated test suites for all scenarios, and (8) creating thorough documentation covering usage, troubleshooting, workflow diagrams, and code review checklist.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves several high-complexity components, including disk-backed caching, overlap-aware concatenation with ffmpeg, integration with existing video workflow logic, robust error handling, and backward compatibility. It is dependent on both system-level and application-level concerns (file I/O, tensor shapes/types, cross-platform reliability), requires coordinating multiple latent formats and video models, and mandates comprehensive testing and documentation. Each step must address numerous edge cases (e.g., None latents, shape mismatches, concurrent execution), making implementation and validation challenging. Full test and CI/CD strategy and documentation add to task scope. The existing subtasks capture logical phases required for both technical correctness and maintainability.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement VideoLatentMask Node for Frame-wise Mask Generation in ComfyUI
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Expand this task into subtasks for: (1) implementing the VideoLatentMask node (including tensor allocation and mask logic), and (2) registering the node, writing unit tests, validating integration, and documenting workflow and parameters.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task is considerably less complex than Task 1. It focuses on creating a new node for tensor mask generation, which is mostly isolated from deeper workflow logic. The main technical challenge is correct tensor manipulation and edge-case handling, with additional requirements for node registration, parameterization, style and type checks, and documentation. While some care is needed to ensure compatibility and test coverage, dependency and integration complexity is low, and testing requirements are straightforward.
